content.text: what happened

woke up and my hands were on fire. both hands, mostly knuckles and wrists. couldn't make a fist. knew it was coming after 3 straight days of nonstop typing but still. should've taken more breaks but i was trying to close out 312 before EOD monday and then carson dropped two more tickets on me.
pain breakdown
right hand knuckles: 7/10
left hand knuckles: 6/10
right wrist: 5/10
left wrist: 4/10
stiffness in both hands, worse in the morning. loosened up a little by noon but still pretty bad
triggers
3-day CSS sprint, barely stopped typing since monday
didn't take real breaks, just powered through
apartment was freezing all week, heat was acting up
stress. carson keeps piling tickets and the sprint deadline is friday
what i did
worked from home. told carson 'not feeling great'
600mg ibuprofen at 8am, again at 2pm
wrist braces on both hands all day
ice packs, 20 min on / 20 min off
saw dr. patel at 2. she said keep up NSAIDs, rest the hands, wants me to reconsider methotrexate. told her i'd think about it.
workout plan this week
no gym until at least friday
swimming thursday and saturday instead of weights
skip morning run tomorrow, maybe a light walk instead
no grip-heavy exercises for at least a week
mood

honestly terrible. the pain makes everything feel heavier. couldn't stop thinking about what happens if this gets worse and i can't code at all. what do i do then. sat through standup pretending everything was fine while my hands were throbbing. carson asked me to pick up another ticket in the same message where he said feel better. he doesn't know so i can't even be mad about it but god. therapy tonight helped a lot. dr. chung called out the catastrophizing spiral, says its textbook for flare days. we did some grounding exercises. going to try to actually sleep tonight.
goal
$20,000 for down payment on a house. started saving sept 2025. doing this for me and mia.
balance
log
sept 2025 - $8,000. starting point. we got this
oct - $7,400. jordan missed payment. had to transfer $600 to cover daycare
nov - $6,800. missed again. $600 transfer. starting to lose hope
dec - $5,200. missed + christmas for mia. transferred $800 total
jan 2026 - $4,400. jordan actually paid this month! still needed $200 for car repair tho
feb 10 - $4,000. jordan didn't pay again. transferred $400 from savings for daycare. tuition going up to $1,000 next month too. at this rate ill never hit 20k
why

i'm tired of being the css girl. carson only gives me styling tickets and the senior devs get all the react work. if i want to grow i need to teach myself and build a portfolio. this is my plan to get there.
the plan
udemy complete react developer course (max schwarzmuller) - enrolled jan 6
finish by end of february
build 2 portfolio projects in march
start applying to react roles by april
key topics
hooks (useState, useEffect, useContext, useReducer), react router, state management
api calls and data fetching, testing with jest + RTL, deployment (vercel or netlify)
idea

recipe finder app. search by ingredient, save favorites, meal planning. something practical that shows i can build a full react app from scratch. mia is a picky eater so maybe this will actually be useful lol
stack
react + vite for build tooling
spoonacular api for recipe data
tailwind css (ironic that i'm using css skills here too)
progress
feb 25 - set up project, basic routing working, homepage layout done
mar 2 - search component done, api integration working. spoonacular free tier is kinda slow but fine for a portfolio piece
mar 9 - favorites feature done with localStorage. this is actually fun
mar 14 - deployed to vercel! looks legit. added some responsive tweaks (lol)
concept
kanban-style task dashboard. drag and drop, priority levels, due dates. this is the project that shows i can handle real state management and complex ui interactions, not just styling
stack
react + vite, zustand for state (trying something besides context), react-beautiful-dnd
progress
mar 18 - initial setup, board layout with three columns (todo, in progress, done)
mar 25 - drag and drop between columns working. zustand makes state so much cleaner than prop drilling
apr 1 - added priority badges and due date warnings. deploying to vercel this week
company

mosaic digital. mid-size agency in midtown atlanta, about 80 people. clients are mostly retail and hospitality brands. tech stack is react, next.js, node. glassdoor reviews are decent, 3.8 stars. hybrid schedule 3 days in office
role
front-end developer. react and next.js. salary range 75-95k. that's a big jump from what i'm making now. sarah chen is the recruiter, phone screen april 8 at 11am
phone screen prep
why leaving brightloop: looking for more growth in react development, want to work on bigger projects (keep it positive, don't vent about carson or the css pigeonhole)
portfolio: recipe finder app (vercel link) and task dashboard (github link). both deployed and working
react experience: 50-hour udemy course completed, two portfolio projects, plus real troubleshooting at work (helped dev with a useEffect cleanup thing in february)
questions for them: team size for the project i'd join, code review process, what a typical sprint looks like, growth path from this role
reminders
stay positive about brightloop, say i want to learn more about the role before discussing salary, have github repo links ready to share
documents needed
w-2 from brightloop (gusto) - got it 2/1
1099-INT from ally bank (savings interest) - got it 2/3
year-end childcare statement from little acorns - got it 2/10
fidelity 401k annual summary - got it 2/2
credits to claim
child and dependent care credit - childcare expenses for mia (form 2441)
child tax credit for mia ($2,000)
file as head of household (bigger standard deduction)
notes
using turbotax again this year. filed last year and it was pretty easy. gonna try to knock it out by mid-feb before i put it off and forget
little acorns total for 2025: $950/mo x 12 = $11,400. need their tax ID number for the childcare credit claim. should be on the year-end statement when it comes
what happened
this whole week was just css tickets. carson dropped 4 more on me tuesday morning and told me to have them done by friday. 7 tickets total. i barely came up for air.
how i feel

mood: 3/10. i feel like a machine that writes css. not a developer, just a pixel-fixer. i had to delete my wednesday focus time to catch up on sprint work. that was my react study time. the one hour i had carved out for myself.
hands
right hand: 3/10. not a full flare but stiff and achey from typing all week
wrists tight. wearing braces tonight
what's eating at me
i'm the css girl. that's all carson sees. 'you're the fastest on this stuff' is not a compliment when it means i never get to touch anything else
dev got to work on the dashboard refactor and notification panel. actual react work. i got hover states and font sizes
portfolio progress this week: zero. didn't touch the recipe app once
i finished everything he asked for and all i got was a thumbs up emoji. cool
plan
this weekend i need to get back to the recipe app. at least get the search component hooked up. i can't let this sprint swallow my entire timeline.
